overview: Orzhov Midrange leverages efficient removal and resilient creatures to stabilize early and outvalue opponents in the mid-to-late game. By playing a balanced curve of threats and answers, the deck aims to survive aggressive starts before deploying larger, game-ending threats. The synergy between creature death triggers and conditional removal creates a flexible game plan that adapts to the opponent's strategy.
win_conditions: Trading efficiently to establish a board presence with resilient creatures
Using removal to clear the path for late-game threats
Generating incremental value through creature death triggers and conditional spells

keep_criteria: Hands with early interaction or a 1-2 drop creature
Balanced mana bases with at least two lands
Combinations of removal and a threat that can play turn 3
mulligan_criteria: Mana screws or heavy land hands
Hands lacking early plays or answers
Overloaded with high-cost cards without early interaction

early_game: Play lands and cheap threats to establish presence
Use early removal to disrupt opponent's game plan
Prioritize card advantage by trading efficiently
mid_game: Deploy signature creatures to apply pressure
Hold removal for key opponent threats
Begin generating value through death triggers and conditional effects
late_game: Stabilize with larger threats and board wipes if necessary
Leverage accumulated card advantage to outlast opponents
Close out games with resilient, hard-to-remove creatures

key_cards.name: Bloodletter of Aclazotz
key_cards.role: Resilient threat that generates value upon death
key_cards.notes: Use as a board presence that pressures opponents while providing incremental damage or card advantage when removed.

key_cards.name: Unstoppable Slasher
key_cards.role: Fast, aggressive creature to apply early pressure
key_cards.notes: Deploy early to force trades or attack directly, leveraging its speed to disrupt opponent's timing.

key_cards.name: Requiting Hex
key_cards.role: Conditional removal to clear opponent's threats
key_cards.notes: Hold for high-value targets or use proactively to maintain board control, ensuring the condition is met for maximum efficiency.

sequencing_tips: Prioritize playing threats before holding removal unless a specific answer is needed
Use creature death triggers to trigger additional effects before casting removal
Manage mana carefully to ensure you can cast both a threat and an answer on the same turn

matchups.advice: Play defensively early, using removal on their biggest threats and holding up lands for answers.
Apply steady pressure with resilient creatures and force them to use multiple removal spells.
Focus on card advantage and efficient trades, looking for windows to deploy threats they cannot answer.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Archetype name and signature cards imply a balanced, value-oriented approach typical of midrange.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Derived from midrange archetype principles and the provided signature cards.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard midrange piloting principles applied to the provided card list.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard format meta trends and midrange archetype interactions.
